support.summary: No CEO-irreplaceable lever; standard EB-access motion
support.detail: DP World is a $250k sole-sourced deal in Formal Evaluation with no blocked discount, no roadmap commitment, and no presales/exec veto in play. The only open risk is that the Economic Buyer is unidentified (Danny Espinoza can scope but not approve $250k, per his 22 Jun 'double check with head office'). Securing the EB introduction is a rep/RSD motion Marc Quessenberry can run on the pricing call; it does not require the CEO's personal availability.
support.why_not_vp: EB introduction and pricing proposal are routine field-motion handled by the AE and their manager; no CEO-to-CEO peer relationship is required at this stage.

monitor.reason: Deal is actively progressing: the pricing 'Metrics for proposal' thread ran through 29 Jun, last activity 7 days ago, close date (10 Dec) unchanged, no competitor.
